value_chain_position: RF/microwave and photonic semiconductors — supplies compound-semiconductor (GaAs/GaN/InP) devices for data-center optical transceivers, 5G base stations, and defense radar/electronic warfare systems.
relative_comps: MACOM has found a niche in InP-based components for 800G/1.6T optical transceivers in AI data centers, a market growing at 40%+ annually. Its defense segment (radar, EW) provides a second AI-adjacent growth vector. Revenue grew 27% YoY, and the company has been taking share from Lumentum and Coherent in the data-center optical component layer. Smaller and less diversified than ADI or QRVO; its compound-semi focus is a genuine moat in high-frequency applications where silicon cannot substitute. Trades at ~40x forward earnings — premium justified by its optical AI tailwind.
